source_intake_record.raw_question_text:
The distance between two shelves in Monika's kitchen is $36 \mathrm{~cm}$. She knows that a stack of 8 identical glasses is $42 \mathrm{~cm}$ high and a stack of 2 such glasses is $18 \mathrm{~cm}$ high. How many glasses has the biggest stack that will fit between two shelves?
<image1>

issues: metadata.image_paths is inconsistent with top-level image_paths

image_dependency: All needed quantities (36 cm, 42 cm for 8 glasses, 18 cm for 2 glasses) are explicitly stated in the text
Although an image is registered, the problem can be solved without visual information
multi_step: The solver must combine two stack-height conditions (8 glasses -> 42 cm and 2 glasses -> 18 cm)
This supports deriving the per-added-glass height increase before evaluating shelf fit
A final maximization step is needed: determine the largest integer stack height not exceeding 36 cm
verifiability: answer_type is numeric and answer_text is a single value
The problem data provides exact measurements, enabling deterministic checking
The result can be verified by reconstructing the stack-height rule and confirming whether 6 fits but 7 does not

risk_flags: metadata.image_paths is inconsistent with top-level image_paths

decision_reasons: Question text and numeric answer are present, so the core semantics are recoverable and verifiable.
Initial scoring indicates strong quality: high multi-step score and very high verifiability score.
The problem appears text-dominant, so the minor image-path metadata inconsistency does not block understanding or solution.
Registry passed and there is no evidence of missing core assets.
